title: Rosa and the Mystery of the Missing Nuts
synopsis: Rosa the curious rabbit discovers that all the nuts in the forest have mysteriously disappeared. She teams up with her friends Marcus the mouse and Zara the zebra finch to solve the case, following clues that lead them to a surprising discovery about their friend Sam the squirrel.

pages.text:
Rosa the rabbit loved mysteries. She had big brown eyes and soft gray fur. Rosa lived in a cozy burrow under the old oak tree.

One sunny morning, Rosa hopped outside. Something felt different in the forest. Very different indeed.

"Where are all the nuts?" Rosa asked herself. She looked around with wide eyes.

illustration.description: Rosa, a small gray rabbit with big expressive brown eyes and a pink nose, stands outside her burrow under a massive oak tree. She looks puzzled and curious. The forest setting has rich greens and golden sunlight filtering through leaves. Scattered empty spots where nuts should be are visible on the ground.
illustration.alt: Rosa the rabbit looking puzzled outside her burrow under an oak tree

pages.text:
Usually, nuts were everywhere. Acorns rolled on the ground. Walnuts sat by the stream. Hazelnuts hung from branches.

But today? Not a single nut anywhere!

"This is very strange," Rosa said. She twitched her nose. "Very strange indeed."

illustration.description: A wide view of the forest showing empty spots where nuts should be. Rosa stands in the center, her ears perked up alertly. The scene shows tree branches, a babbling stream, and patches of grass - all conspicuously missing any nuts. Bright, saturated forest colors with dappled sunlight.
illustration.alt: Empty forest scene with Rosa looking around for missing nuts

pages.text:
Rosa heard crying nearby. She followed the sound with her ears up high.

Behind a mushroom, she found Marcus. He was a tiny brown mouse with round glasses. Tears rolled down his furry cheeks.

"Marcus! What's wrong?" Rosa asked gently.

illustration.description: Marcus, a small brown mouse wearing tiny round glasses, sits crying behind a large red-spotted mushroom. Rosa approaches with concern, her ears forward and eyes kind. The mushroom is bright red with white spots, surrounded by soft moss and small flowers. Warm, comforting lighting.
illustration.alt: Marcus the mouse crying behind a mushroom while Rosa approaches

pages.text:
"All the nuts are gone!" Marcus sobbed. "I was saving acorns for my little sister's birthday. Now they're all missing!"

Rosa patted Marcus's tiny paw. "Don't worry. We'll solve this mystery together."

Just then, Zara the zebra finch landed on Rosa's head.

illustration.description: Rosa comforts Marcus by gently patting his paw. Marcus looks sad but hopeful. Zara, a small black and white striped finch with bright orange beak, perches on Rosa's head with her wings spread. The scene is warm and friendly with soft afternoon lighting and colorful wildflowers around them.
illustration.alt: Rosa comforting Marcus while Zara the zebra finch lands on Rosa's head

pages.text:
"Hi friends!" chirped Zara. She had black and white stripes and a bright orange beak. "Why does Marcus look so sad?"

"Someone took all the nuts in the forest," Rosa explained.

Zara's eyes grew wide. "All of them? That's terrible!"

illustration.description: Close-up of the three friends together. Zara looks shocked with her beak slightly open and big round eyes. Rosa looks determined and thoughtful. Marcus pushes up his glasses with a worried expression. Rich, warm colors with the friends clustered together showing their strong friendship.
illustration.alt: The three friends discussing the missing nuts with various expressions

pages.text:
"We need clues," Rosa said. She put on her thinking face. "Let's look around carefully."

The three friends spread out. They searched high and low. Under rocks. Behind trees. Near the pond.

"Look!" called Marcus. "I found something!"

illustration.description: The three friends searching in different areas of the forest. Rosa sniffs near some rocks, Marcus peers behind a tree trunk, and Zara flies overhead scanning the area. Marcus points excitedly at something he's discovered. The scene shows their teamwork with dynamic poses and bright, encouraging colors.
illustration.alt: The friends searching for clues throughout the forest

pages.text:
Marcus pointed to tiny scratches on a tree. "These marks are fresh," he said, pushing up his glasses.

Zara flew higher. "I see a trail!" she called down. "It leads to the big hill!"

Rosa's ears perked up. "Let's follow it!"

illustration.description: Marcus examines small claw marks on tree bark through his glasses, looking like a tiny detective. Zara hovers above pointing with her wing toward a hill in the distance. Rosa looks excited and ready for adventure. The scene has golden hour lighting with the hill visible in the background through the trees.
illustration.alt: Marcus examining clues while Zara spots a trail leading to the hill

pages.text:
The friends followed the trail up the hill. Rosa hopped quickly. Marcus rode on her back. Zara flew circles above them.

At the top, they heard strange noises. "Clink, clunk, roll, roll."

"What could that be?" whispered Rosa.

illustration.description: The three friends climbing a grassy hill together. Rosa bounds up with Marcus holding onto her fur, looking adventurous. Zara swoops playfully overhead. The hilltop ahead shows mysterious shadows and the source of strange sounds. Vibrant greens and blues with an air of mystery and excitement.
illustration.alt: The friends climbing the hill following the mysterious trail

pages.text:
They peeked over the hill. There sat Sam the squirrel! He had fluffy red fur and was surrounded by mountains of nuts.

But Sam wasn't eating them. He was... organizing them?

"Red nuts here. Brown nuts there. Small ones in this pile," Sam muttered.

illustration.description: Sam, a fluffy red squirrel with a bushy tail, sits in the center of elaborate, organized piles of different nuts - sorted by size and color. He looks focused and methodical. The three friends peek over the hill crest in the background, their eyes wide with surprise and curiosity. Warm sunset colors illuminate the scene.
illustration.alt: Sam the squirrel organizing huge piles of nuts while the friends watch

pages.text:
"Sam!" called Rosa. "Why did you take all the nuts?"

Sam looked up, surprised. His cheeks turned pink. "Oh! Hi everyone. I... I was planning something special."

"Special?" asked Marcus. "What kind of special?"

illustration.description: Sam looks embarrassed and shy, his paws fidgeting and cheeks blushed pink. The three friends approach him with curious but friendly expressions. The organized nut piles create a colorful backdrop. The mood shifts from mystery to understanding, with warm, forgiving lighting.
illustration.alt: Sam looking embarrassed as the friends confront him about the nuts

pages.text:
Sam's tail twitched nervously. "Tomorrow is the Forest Fair. I wanted to make a giant nut sculpture for everyone to enjoy!"

He pointed to his drawings. "See? A nut castle! But I was too shy to ask for help."

Rosa smiled. "That sounds wonderful!"

illustration.description: Sam holds up sketches of an elaborate nut castle design with towers and walls made of different nuts. His expression changes from nervous to hopeful. The friends look at the drawings with growing excitement and admiration. The scene is filled with creative energy and friendship, with the sketches detailed and whimsical.
illustration.alt: Sam showing his castle designs to the excited friends

pages.text:
"We'll help you build it!" said Marcus excitedly.

"It will be the best sculpture ever!" chirped Zara.

Sam's eyes lit up with joy. "Really? You'd help me?"

"Friends always help friends," Rosa said with a big smile.

illustration.description: All four friends embrace in a group hug, looking absolutely delighted. Sam's eyes sparkle with happiness and relief. The organized nut piles surround them, ready to become art. Golden sunset light bathes the scene with warmth and friendship. Their expressions show pure joy and the power of teamwork.
illustration.alt: The four friends hugging happily, ready to work together

pages.text:
The next day, the Forest Fair was amazing! Sam's nut castle stood tall and proud. All the animals cheered and clapped.

"Mystery solved!" laughed Rosa. "And we made a new friend too!"

The four friends shared acorn cookies and smiled together.

illustration.description: A beautiful nut castle made of colorful sorted nuts stands as the centerpiece of a festive forest fair. Various diverse forest animals - deer, raccoons, bears, rabbits, birds of different species - admire the sculpture. The four friends stand proudly together in front of their creation, beaming with accomplishment. Festive banners and decorations complete the joyful celebration scene.
illustration.alt: The completed nut castle at the forest fair with all the animals celebrating

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was missing from the forest?
questions.explanation: All the nuts in the forest had mysteriously disappeared, which started Rosa's investigation.

questions.question: Who was crying behind the mushroom?
questions.explanation: Marcus the mouse was crying because he had been saving acorns for his little sister's birthday.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Zara?
questions.explanation: Zara is a zebra finch - a small bird with black and white stripes and a bright orange beak.

questions.question: Why did Sam take all the nuts?
questions.explanation: Sam wanted to create a giant nut castle sculpture for the Forest Fair, but was too shy to ask for help.

questions.question: How did the story end?
questions.explanation: The friends all worked together to build Sam's nut castle, and it was the highlight of the Forest Fair.

pages.title: Complete Rosa's Detective Notes
pages.instructions: Help Rosa fill in her notes about the mystery!
pages.text: Today I solved the mystery of the missing ___. My friend ___ was very sad because his sister's birthday acorns were gone. ___ helped us search from above, and we found ___ organizing all the nuts on the hill. He wanted to make a beautiful ___ for the Forest Fair!

pages.title: What Happened Next?
pages.instructions: Put the story events in order by filling in the blanks!
pages.text: First, Rosa discovered all the ___ were missing. Then she found Marcus ___ behind a mushroom. Next, ___ flew overhead and spotted a trail. The friends followed the trail up the ___ where they found Sam. Finally, they all worked together to build a wonderful ___ for the fair!
